vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of severe degradation. The expected specimens (P1-P4) are not visible as healthy, identifiable plants. The yellow pot (P1) and black pot (P3/P4) contain only soil and debris.

plant_audit.P1_String_of_Nickels: No viable foliage detected. Soil surface appears barren.
plant_audit.P2_Mexican_Mint: No viable foliage detected. Pot appears empty of living matter.
plant_audit.P3_Pothos: The white rabbit anchor is present, but the Pothos specimen is absent or entirely desiccated/submerged in soil debris.
plant_audit.P4_Silver_Guest: No evidence of the silver guest plant; likely lost to systemic failure.

biome_observations.soil_condition: Dark, damp, and potentially anaerobic. No signs of active growth.
biome_observations.debris: Presence of white, fragmented material (likely eggshell or mineral deposits) in the black pot.
biome_observations.surface_anomalies: The desk surface is clear of organic matter; no incidental growth or moss detected.

vision_report.temporal_deltas: Over the 5-day sequence, there is zero positive growth. The state of the pots remains static and devoid of biological activity, confirming a total loss of the registered specimens.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue and the lack of structural integrity in the soil suggest the plants have perished.
vision_report.anomalies: The white rabbit anchor remains as a static marker in a graveyard of soil. No new introductions or interventions observed.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a meticulous audit of the provided image stack. My first action was to establish the baseline against the registry; my validation confirms that all registered plants (P1-P4) have suffered a total systemic loss. The environment is currently a sterile soil-based setup with no active botanical life. The white rabbit anchor is the only non-soil element remaining in the black pot. The biome is currently in a state of total failure.
